content:
Hi Tombarkas

What do you mean "It makes no difference". Could you detail what your exceptions are versus what your observing.

Unified inbox folder

The only area where the action is limited is within the unified inbox folder. There you must open the email first before moving it. Moving from the message list is not available.

  1. Open email
  2. Press 3 dot menu
  3. Select Move, or refile > Move

For other accounts & folders

From the message list view

  1. Select message(s)
  2. Press 3 dot menu
  3. Select move.

From an open email.

  1. Press 3 dot menu
  2. Select Move, or refile > Move

Swipe

The swipe operation also appears to work for the move operation. Swiping displays folder list allowing you to choose where to move the email to.

content:
Hi Tombarkas

There was a developer response (CKetti) to the same issue reported by a user in March 2024. I recognize it's not the answer you hoped for.

"Did you set up your account using POP3? Not being able to move messages from the Trash folder to the Inbox is a known limitation because the app doesn't properly support local folders or messages (see #648).

For the foreseeable future the focus of the app will be to manage email on the server. Try using IMAP instead."

Statement Reference https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/7733#issuecomment-2017658423

You can create a feature request on Mozilla Connect where the community can vote for the idea. https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/idb-p/ideas/label-name/thunderbird%20android
creator: platform34

content:
Can you provide greater detail please.

What version of the Android app are you running and who is your email provider.

Have you completed the first time setup after app installation and successfully added an email account?

How did you complete the account addition? Manually by entering email & completing authentication or by scanning QR code from Thunderbird desktop?

Within the app, can you navigate to the following setting pages.

Settings > account > fetching mail > incoming server Settings > account > sending mail > outgoing server

Please click next on each of these pages, which will test connection to and authentication with the server? Does each complete successfully or report an error?

Do you know if your account is using Imap or pop3 connections?
